The Sales Objection: We can't send our PII to OpenAI public API.
The Winning Response (Solution): We deploy via Azure OpenAI Service within your private VPC; zero data retention.
Refined Source: Microsoft Azure, "Data, privacy, and security for Azure OpenAI Service," 2024.

The Sales Objection: If the AI copies code, are we liable for copyright?
The Winning Response (Solution): We offer a 'Copyright Shield' indemnity policy to cover any legal claims.

The Sales Objection: I don't trust it not to lie to my best customers.
The Winning Response (Solution): We use RAG (Retrieval) to ground every answer in your verified PDFs only.

The Sales Objection: This is going to bankrupt us at scale.
The Winning Response (Solution): We use a 'Router' to send easy queries to cheap models (GPT-3.5) and hard ones to GPT-4.
Refined Source: Databricks, "The CIO's Guide to Generative AI," 2024.

The Sales Objection: My team thinks this tool is here to fire them.
The Winning Response (Solution): Position it as a 'Co-pilot' that handles the boring repetitive tickets, not the complex ones.
Refined Source: HBR, "Reskilling in the Age of AI," 2023.

The Sales Objection: It cited a fake law in our test.
The Winning Response (Solution): We implement 'Citations' so users can click to verify the source document instantly.

The Sales Objection: Show me the money. Where is the revenue lift?
The Winning Response (Solution): Start with a pilot on 'Internal Search' (Productivity) before 'Customer Facing' (Risk).
Refined Source: McKinsey Digital, "The Economic Potential of Generative AI," 2023.

The Sales Objection: Our data is a mess; the AI won't learn anything.
The Winning Response (Solution): We don't need to retrain the model; we just need to index your clean documents (RAG).

The Sales Objection: It takes 5 seconds to answer; users will churn.
The Winning Response (Solution): We can switch to a 'Groq' inference chip or a smaller, faster model (Llama 3 8B).

The Sales Objection: Users can trick it into revealing system prompts.
The Winning Response (Solution): We use a separate 'Guardrail Model' to scan every input before it hits the LLM.

The Sales Objection: Is this compliant with the new EU AI Act?
The Winning Response (Solution): Yes, we classify as a 'General Purpose AI' with full transparency logs and opt-out.
Refined Source: European Parliament, "EU AI Act: Compliance Guide," 2024.

The Sales Objection: I don't want to be stuck with just OpenAI.
The Winning Response (Solution): Our architecture is 'Model Agnostic'—you can swap GPT-4 for Claude 3 with one config change.

The Sales Objection: We tried a chatbot in 2018 and it sucked.
The Winning Response (Solution): 2018 was 'Pattern Matching' (NLP). 2024 is 'Reasoning' (LLMs). Let me show you the difference.

The Sales Objection: We don't have 6 months to integrate this.
The Winning Response (Solution): Because it's pre-trained, we only need 2 weeks to connect your Knowledge Base (APIs).
Refined Source: Y Combinator, "Startup School: Building with LLMs," 2023.

The Sales Objection: What if this leaks our trade secrets?
The Winning Response (Solution): We use 'Zero Data Retention' policies—the model processes the data but never learns from it.
